title: 'Such carnage defies belief': Lebanon, crushed by Israeli bombs, counts its dead
subhead: A few hours after the announcement of a ceasefire between the US and Iran, around 100 airstrikes targeted several neighborhoods in central Beirut, its southern suburbs, and in towns and villages in eastern and southern Lebanon on Wednesday. At least 254 people were killed, according to the civil defense.

title: Trump blasts NATO after closed-door meeting with Secretary General Rutte
subhead: The US president accused allies of 'turning their back' on the American people after Washington's partners refused to back his war on Iran. After his meeting on Wednesday, he attacked NATO on social media and appeared to renew threats over Greenland.

title: Four migrants die trying to cross Channel to England, say French authorities
subhead: The two men and two women were swept away by the current near the northern village of Equihen-Plage, while another person was treated for hypothermia.

title: Nearly half of French people say they've been victims of racism, survey finds
subhead: Exposure to racist violence and discrimination was overwhelmingly reported among visible minorities. Religious affiliation was identified as the second major factor of exposure.

title: World Press Photo 2026: Abdulmonam Eassa honored for his coverage of the war in Sudan for Le Monde
subhead: French-Syrian photojournalist Abdulmonam Eassa received the prestigious World Press Photo award on Thursday in the 'Stories' category for his photo report 'War in Sudan, A Nation Trapped,' carried out in October 2024 and December 2025 for Le Monde.

Israel's rule of law and democracy are on the brink of collapse
subhead: The introduction of the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of 'a terrorist act,' but not for Israeli Jews guilty of abuses and the murder of civilians in the occupied West Bank, is emblematic of the ongoing transformation of Israel.

title: In catacombs, museums, and labs, the delicate heritage of human remains
subhead: The Paris Catacombs reopen to the public on Wednesday, April 8, after five months of renovations. The timing offers a chance to reflect on how human remains are handled in cultural and research institutions. These singular relics pose ethical, technical and legal questions.

title: How the Strait of Hormuz became Iran's secret weapon in the war with the US and Israel
subhead: Despite Donald Trump's ultimatum, Tehran does not appear willing to reopen the maritime passage, which is of strategic importance for the global economy. The regime has made the strait a tool of national sovereignty and is even considering charging for passage once the conflict is over.

title: 'Iran is caught in a vice between two regimes of silence: war and repression'
